The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics’ Occupational Outlook Handbook entry “Barbers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists” supplies nationwide job-growth projections, median pay, work settings, and employment-change factors (federal outlook/salary data) [1].  
Washington State Department of Licensing’s “Cosmetologists, Barbers, Manicurists and Estheticians” pages post official requirements for training hours, exams, reciprocity, renewals, and downloadable application forms for practitioners in Washington (state-level licensing) [2].  
U.S. News & World Report’s “Barber, Hairstylist and Cosmetologist – Career Ranking” profile blends labor data with journalist interviews of working stylists who share tips on customer relations, portfolio building, and career advancement (news-based advice) [3].  

Books for career preparation/management:  
• Milady Standard Cosmetology 2016 (Milady; ISBN 9781285769417) – comprehensive textbook used in schools; covers technical skills, state-board prep, and professional ethics.  
• The Business of Beauty: Your Guide to Starting a Successful Beauty Business by Lauren M. Snow (ISBN 9781733754603) – offers step-by-step planning, branding, finance, and client-retention strategies for cosmetology entrepreneurs.  

Multimedia insight: the “Cosmetologist” video segment in the Occupational Outlook Handbook Video Library gives a two-minute, on-the-job look at cutting, coloring, and client interaction inside an active salon (profession overview via video) [4].  

Together, these authoritative resources span federal statistics, state licensure, practitioner perspectives, in-depth study texts, entrepreneurial guidance, and visual workplace context—covering key informational needs for anyone exploring or advancing a cosmetology career.

## References  
[1] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, Occupational Outlook Handbook: Barbers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists, bls.gov, 2024.  
[2] Washington State Department of Licensing, Cosmetologists, Barbers, Manicurists and Estheticians, dol.wa.gov, 2024.  
[3] U.S. News & World Report, Barber, Hairstylist and Cosmetologist Career Profile, usnews.com, 2024.  
[4] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, Occupational Outlook Handbook Video Library – Cosmetologist, bls.gov/video, 2024.
rounds.answer_b:
The **U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) Occupational Outlook Handbook** provides federal employment outlook and salary data for barbers, hairstylists, and cosmetologists, including median pay, projected job growth, and educational requirements [1]. This is the primary authoritative source for national workforce statistics in this occupation.

For state-level licensing, the **Washington State Department of Licensing (DOL)** provides official requirements for cosmetology professionals, including examination prerequisites, training-hour mandates, and license renewal procedures specific to Washington State [2].

A national news-based career profile is offered by **U.S. News & World Report's "Best Jobs" series**, which features a detailed career profile for hairstylists and cosmetologists, including practitioner advice, workplace satisfaction ratings, and career advancement insights [3].

Two essential career-preparation and management books in cosmetology include: (1) **"Milady Standard Cosmetology"** by Milady (ISBN: 978-1285769417), the industry-standard educational textbook covering all foundational cosmetology skills and theory [4]; and (2) **"Salon Business Strategies: A New Approach"** by Robert Croslin (ISBN: 978-1111640668), which addresses salon business management, marketing, and client retention strategies for professionals [5].

For multimedia insight, the **CareerOneStop Video Library**, sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor, offers video profiles of cosmetologists and hairstylists at work, presenting day-in-the-life footage, practitioner interviews, and discussions of skills and working conditions [6].

Together, these resources comprehensively cover federal data, state licensing, practitioner guidance, educational texts, and visual career exploration within cosmetology.

## References

[1] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. "Barbers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists." Occupational Outlook Handbook. https://www.bls.gov/ooh/personal-care-and-service/barbers-hairstylists-and-cosmetologists.htm

[2] Washington State Department of Licensing. "Cosmetology Licensing." https://www.dol.wa.gov/business/cosmetology/

[3] U.S. News & World Report. "Hairstylist Career Profile." https://money.usnews.com/careers/best-jobs/hairstylist

[4] Milady. *Milady Standard Cosmetology*, 13th Edition. Cengage Learning, 2015. ISBN: 978-1285769417.

[5] Croslin, Robert. *Salon Business Strategies: A New Approach*. Cengage Learning, 2012. ISBN: 978-1111640668.

[6] CareerOneStop. "Cosmetologists Video." U.S. Department of Labor. https://www.careeronestop.org/Videos/CareerVideos/career-videos.aspx

The subject librarian responsible for the cosmetology career research guide at SFCC is **Barbara Eng**. She can be reached by phone at **(509) 533-3595**. This alphabetical three-range organizational system allows students to quickly navigate to their specific career interest area and access profession-relevant eBooks, making the library's digital collection both accessible and logically structured for career exploration across all programs offered at the college [1][2].

Washington State’s Department of Licensing (DOL) regulates the profession; its Cosmetologists, Barbers, Manicurists and Estheticians portal outlines required training hours, written / practical exams, renewals, reciprocity, fees, and online forms, making it the authoritative source for state licensing and maintenance [2].

Key career and exam-preparation books:  
• Milady Standard Cosmetology 2016, Milady / Cengage; ISBN 9781285769417 – full curriculum text covering theory, procedures, infection control, and professional ethics.  
• Milady Standard Cosmetology Exam Review, Milady / Cengage; ISBN 9781285769554 – 1,000-plus multiple-choice questions with rationales that mirror state-board exams.
